prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde were computed with the chemkit fukui-reactivity skill using DFT, tier “standard” (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in the gas phase.  The calculation performed three single-point SCFs on the user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz geometry: the neutral closed-shell (charge 0, multiplicity 1), the cation doublet (charge +1, mult 2) and the anion doublet (charge –1, mult 2).  Mulliken charges from those three states give the Fukui functions; the headline electrophilic index (f⁺) on the carbonyl carbon is 

    f⁺(C) = 0.7083913 (unitless)

The oxygen atom is the most nucleophilic site with f⁻(O) = 0.46742464.

Method provenance block
• Property: Fukui f⁺/f⁻/dual descriptor (Mulliken charge finite-difference)
• Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted) single points (`DFT//provided geometry`)
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied /…/formaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization)
• Solvent: gas phase (no implicit model)
• Charge/multiplicity: 0 1 (neutral), +1 2 (cation), –1 2 (anion)
• Software: chemkit-PySCF backend v2024-06 snapshot
• Convergence: all three SCFs converged; ∑f⁺ and ∑f⁻ both equal 1.0000 (charge conservation checks passed)
• Warnings: none returned

Reproducibility: command recorded in result JSON (`cli_invocation` field); live engine log was written to the default `<cwd>/fukui_*.out` path during the run.  Optimised charges and full per-atom f⁺/f⁻/f⁰ arrays, dual descriptor, and the PNG reactivity plot are in the result JSON and at `/Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/20260625-114829_formaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/`.

No experimental references are provided because Fukui indices are a purely computational quantity.
